The oath for Mayor of New York City is supposed to affirm loyalty to the U.S. Constitution and the Constitution of the State of New York. If that oath was not taken properly, then it calls the legitimacy of the office into question. This isn’t about personal belief—it’s about upholding the laws of the United States. An oath that doesn’t meet constitutional standards isn’t symbolic; it matters. Without a valid oath, the authority of the position itself is undermined. What a joke
